Normalization Autoselect Evaluation Report

Generated on 2026-03-02 14:23:20 | ScpTensor v0.1.0-beta | Evaluation Framework

1. Test Overview

Total Scenarios 108
Scales small (100 cells × 500 features), medium (500 cells × 1000 features)
Missing Rates low (20%), medium (40%), high (60%)
Distributions normal, log_normal, multimodal
Normalization Methods norm_mean, norm_median, norm_quantile
Evaluation Metrics intragroup_variation, intergroup_variation, correlation_preservation

2. Key Findings

  • norm_mean is selected in 90.7% of all scenarios (98 out of 108), demonstrating excellent overall performance
  • norm_median shows competitive performance in log_normal distribution scenarios, particularly with moderate missing rates
  • norm_quantile excels in high missing rate (60%) scenarios, providing better variance stabilization
  • All methods maintain high correlation preservation (>0.95) across different data characteristics
  • Method selection is primarily driven by intragroup variation metric, with norm_mean consistently minimizing within-group variance

3. Method Selection Distribution

Figure 1: Distribution of selected normalization methods across 108 test scenarios. norm_mean dominates across most data characteristics.

4. Method Comparison by Metrics

Figure 2: Radar chart showing normalized performance scores across evaluation metrics. Higher values indicate better performance.

5. Performance by Distribution Type

Figure 3: Method performance comparison across different underlying data distributions.

6. Comprehensive Performance Heatmap

Figure 4: Heatmap showing method performance across scale × missing rate × distribution combinations. Darker colors indicate better performance.

7. Recommendations by Scenario

Scenario Recommended Method Reason
Normal distribution norm_mean Best intragroup variation reduction (lowest within-group variance)
Log-normal distribution norm_mean / norm_median Similar performance; norm_mean slightly better for correlation preservation
Multimodal distribution norm_mean Consistently outperforms across all scales and missing rates
High missing rate (60%) norm_quantile Better variance stabilization with extreme sparsity
Low missing rate (20%) norm_mean Optimal balance of all metrics
Small dataset (100 cells) norm_mean Robust performance regardless of distribution type
Large dataset (500+ cells) norm_mean Scalable with consistent results
Default / Unknown distribution norm_mean Safe default with 90.7% selection rate across diverse scenarios

8. Summary

Best Overall Method norm_mean
Selection Rate 90.7% (98/108 scenarios)
Alternative for High Sparsity norm_quantile (high missing rate scenarios)
Robustness All methods maintain correlation preservation >0.95
Recommendation Use autoselect with norm_mean as default fallback
